name: Argilla
tagline: Build high-quality datasets for your AI models with human and AI feedback
seoTitle: Argilla - Data Annotation Platform for LLMs
metaDescription: Argilla is an open-source platform for annotating, reviewing, and curating datasets to train, fine-tune, and evaluate language and NLP models.
overview: Argilla is an open-source collaboration tool for building and curating datasets used to train, fine-tune, and evaluate AI models. It pairs a web UI for human reviewers with a Python SDK, so AI engineers and domain experts can work on the same data through annotation, review, filtering, and semantic search.

It is aimed at teams that care about data quality rather than treating their tooling as a black box. You keep ownership of both your data and your models, and you drive labeling workflows programmatically so they can run as part of continuous evaluation and model improvement.

Within the synthetic data and labeling space, Argilla covers traditional NLP tasks (text classification, NER), LLM workflows (RAG, preference tuning), and multimodal projects. Note: the original authors have stepped back; the project is described as mature and stable, with bug fixes and patches but no new features planned.
features: Web UI plus a Python SDK so domain experts and engineers can label and review the same datasets
Faster labeling with filters, semantic search, and AI feedback suggestions
Supports text classification, NER, RAG, preference tuning, and multimodal data tasks
Programmatic workflows for continuous evaluation and model improvement
Self-hosted: deploy on Hugging Face Spaces or with Docker, keeping data and models under your control
Used in real projects to build open datasets like the cleaned UltraFeedback set

gettingStarted.intro: Install the Python SDK, deploy an Argilla Server, then connect the client to it.

steps.heading: Install the SDK
steps.body: Install the argilla package with pip.

steps.heading: Deploy the server
steps.body: Deploy Argilla Server on Hugging Face Spaces (the easiest option, ready in a few minutes) or self-host with Docker. Your API key is on the My Settings page of your Space, and the API URL is the Space URL ending in .hf.space.

steps.heading: Connect the client
steps.body: Initialize the Argilla client with your api_url and api_key, then call client.me to confirm the connection.
steps.code:
import argilla as rg

client = rg.Argilla(
    api_url="<api_url>",
    api_key="<api_key>"
)

useCases: Collect and review human feedback to build preference datasets for fine-tuning LLMs
Label text for traditional NLP tasks such as classification and named entity recognition
Curate and clean existing datasets by filtering and inspecting examples in the UI
Let domain experts and AI engineers collaborate on the same data for RAG or multimodal projects
